uses: Calawaya caulina,  a plant native to South America, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes. Its roots and leaves are used to prepare infusions and teas that are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is also used to treat digestive issues and reduce fever. Additionally, Calawaya caulina is thought to have antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal uses and potential benefits.
